description: 40-record audience-boundary fixture for CustomerTypeResolver regression testing (TEST-031). Covers difficult classification boundaries: corporate context from personal domains, social occasions from corporate domains, agency+social, luxury language, private/family office, PA/EA bookings, client thank-you edge cases, event-manager wording, and helping-a-friend vs on-behalf-of-client.

records.description: Board meeting requested via Gmail — Rule 2b fires before Rule 5
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I need to book a room for a board meeting for 8 people next Thursday morning.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'board meeting' in consumer-domain email

records.description: Client dinner from Hotmail — Rule 2b fires before Rule 5
records.enquiry_text: Hello, could you check availability for a client dinner for 12 on the 15th?
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'client dinner' in consumer-domain email

records.description: Work team meal from Yahoo — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Hi there, we need a table for a work team meal next Wednesday for 9 people.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'work team meal'

records.description: Managing director lunch from Outlook — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: I'm booking lunch for our managing director and 6 guests on the 22nd.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'managing director'

records.description: Business breakfast from Gmail — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Good morning, do you have availability for a business breakfast for 10 on 8 July?
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'business breakfast'

records.description: Birthday dinner from IBM — Rule 2c overrides Rule 3
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I'm looking for dinner for a birthday for 12 people on 25 July.
records.notes: Social signal 'birthday' in corporate domain — Rule 2c fires before Rule 3

records.description: Baby shower from Microsoft — Rule 2c overrides Rule 3
records.enquiry_text: Hello, do you have lunch availability for a baby shower for 15 guests?
records.notes: Social signal 'baby shower' from known corporate domain

records.description: Engagement party from Deloitte — Rule 2c overrides Rule 3
records.enquiry_text: Hi, could you do dinner for an engagement party for 20 on the 30th?
records.notes: Social signal 'engagement party' from known corporate domain

records.description: Hen party from Google — Rule 2c overrides Rule 3
records.enquiry_text: Hi there, I'm organising a hen do dinner for 14 girls on 10 August.
records.notes: Social signal 'hen do' from known corporate domain

records.description: Graduation dinner from Accenture — Rule 2c overrides Rule 3
records.enquiry_text: Hello, we're planning a graduation dinner for 18 people to celebrate my son's degree.
records.notes: Social signal 'graduation dinner' from known corporate domain

records.description: Agency booking client birthday — Rule 2 fires before Rule 2c
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I'm arranging a birthday dinner for my client and 10 guests on 20 July.
records.notes: Agency signal 'my client' fires before social signal 'birthday'

records.description: Agency venue find for client leaving do — Rule 2 wins
records.enquiry_text: Hello, I'm doing a venue find for a client's leaving party for 20 people.
records.notes: Agency signal 'venue find' fires before social signal 'leaving party'

records.description: Our client baby shower via consumer domain — Rule 2 wins
records.enquiry_text: Good morning, could you help with a baby shower booking for our client on 15 August?
records.notes: Agency signal 'our client' fires before social signal 'baby shower'

records.description: Commission request for stag do — Rule 2 wins
records.enquiry_text: Hi, we're interested in commission for a stag do booking for 12 on 5 July.
records.notes: Agency signal 'commission' fires before social signal 'stag do'

records.description: Known agency domain with hen party — Rule 1 wins
records.enquiry_text: Hi, please can you help with a hen party booking for one of our clients?
records.notes: Known agency domain (Rule 1) fires before any text signals

records.description: High-value proposal dinner from Gmail — Rule 5 (consumer domain, no social signal)
records.enquiry_text: Good evening, I would like to reserve your private dining room for a very special proposal dinner for two.
records.notes: Luxury language but no specific social signal in _SOCIAL_CONTEXT_SIGNALS; consumer domain resolves to social

records.description: 50th birthday luxury dinner from Hotmail — Rule 2c fires
records.enquiry_text: Dear team, I am planning a milestone 50th birthday dinner in your private dining room for 16 guests.
records.notes: Social signal 'birthday' triggers Rule 2c even with luxury-style language

records.description: Wedding anniversary dinner — Rule 2c fires
records.enquiry_text: I would like to book an exclusive private room for our wedding anniversary dinner for 8.
records.notes: Social signal 'wedding anniversary'

records.description: Exclusive birthday celebration from Gmail — Rule 2c fires
records.enquiry_text: Hello, I am looking to book an exclusive birthday dinner for a small group of 6.
records.notes: Social signal 'birthday' despite luxury framing

records.description: Private christening lunch from personal email — Rule 2c fires
records.enquiry_text: Dear Reservations, I am hoping to arrange a private christening lunch for 22 guests in July.
records.notes: Social signal 'christening' — luxury tone does not change classification

records.description: Private family office booking from unknown domain — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Good afternoon, I am booking on behalf of a private family office and need a private room for a team dinner.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'family office'

records.description: Private office in Mayfair from Gmail — Rule 2b overrides Rule 5
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I work at a private office in Mayfair and need to book lunch for 10 next week.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'private office' from consumer domain

records.description: Family office from test dataset domain — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Hello, we are a family office based in London and would like to book a corporate dinner for 14.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'family office' plus 'corporate dinner'

records.description: PA booking for managing director — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I am the PA to our managing director and I need to book a private lunch for 8 on 12 August.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'managing director'

records.description: EA booking for client workshop from Gmail — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Hello, I am an executive assistant and I need to book a room for a client workshop for 16.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'client workshop'

records.description: EA booking board meeting from Outlook — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Good morning, I am booking on behalf of our CEO for a board meeting breakfast for 7.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'board meeting'

records.description: Personal assistant for team meeting from Gmail — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I am a personal assistant booking a team meeting lunch for 12 next Friday.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'team meeting'

records.description: Client thank-you lunch from Gmail — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I need to book a client thank-you lunch for 8 on 3 July.
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'client thank-you'

records.description: Client thank you meal (no hyphen) from Hotmail — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Hello, could you arrange a client thank you meal for our key account team of 10?
records.notes: Corporate context signal 'client thank you' (without hyphen)

records.description: Plain thank-you dinner with no client context from Gmail — Rule 5 (no corporate/social text signal)
records.enquiry_text: Hi, we want to do a thank you dinner for the team on Friday, nothing special.
records.notes: No 'client' prefix — 'thank you dinner' alone does not trigger Rule 2b; consumer domain → social

records.description: Client thank you birthday edge case — Rule 2b fires before Rule 2c
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I'd like to book a client thank you birthday dinner for one of our top clients.
records.notes: Rule 2b (client thank you) fires before Rule 2c (birthday) in precedence order

records.description: Event manager booking for team meeting from unknown domain — Rule 2b fires
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I am an event manager helping to organise a team meeting dinner for 20 on 18 July.
records.notes: 'event manager' alone does not trigger agency; corporate context signal 'team meeting' fires

records.description: Freelance event manager organising birthday — Rule 2c fires
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I'm a freelance event manager and I'm organising a birthday dinner for 14 on the 20th.
records.notes: Event manager + birthday: no commission signal, social signal 'birthday' fires via Rule 2c

records.description: Event manager booking for my client — Rule 2 fires
records.enquiry_text: Hello, I am an event manager and I am booking this dinner for my client for 18 guests.
records.notes: Agency signal 'my client' present in event manager enquiry

records.description: Events manager seeking venue on behalf of our client — Rule 2 fires
records.enquiry_text: Good morning, I am an events manager seeking a private dining venue on behalf of our client for 22.
records.notes: Agency signal 'our client' fires; 'on behalf of our client' also in signal list

records.description: Helping a friend with birthday — Rule 2c fires
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I am helping a friend organise her birthday dinner for 10 on 15 August.
records.notes: Social signal 'birthday' fires; 'a friend' is not a commission signal

records.description: Organising baby shower for colleague — Rule 2c fires
records.enquiry_text: Hello, I am helping to organise a baby shower lunch for a colleague who is leaving next month.
records.notes: Social signal 'baby shower' fires; 'colleague' is not a commission signal

records.description: Booking on behalf of my client — Rule 2 fires
records.enquiry_text: Hi, I am booking this on behalf of my client who needs a private dinner for 12 on 9 July.
records.notes: Agency signal 'my client' — 'on behalf of my client' is effectively 'my client'

records.description: Colleague's leaving party — Rule 2c fires
records.enquiry_text: Hi there, I am helping to sort a leaving do for a colleague who is moving abroad.
records.notes: Social signal 'leaving do' fires; colleague context does not trigger Rule 2

records.description: Generic dinner enquiry from unknown domain — no text signal, no domain match
records.enquiry_text: Hello, we are looking for a private dining room for 10 people next month.
records.notes: No agency, corporate, or social text signals; unknown domain; extraction unknown → Rule 8
